EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 104 actions; a strong score needs about 32 or fewer. You are 3.2x over the target.
NET-ZERO WASTE: your last 104 actions returned the board to a state already seen 104 actions ago — that round-trip made no progress yet still cost 104 actions. Do not extend-and-retract or oscillate; a sequence that ends where it began is pure waste.

The game is over. I've used 104 actions and I'm 3.2x over the target. The game is over, so I can't make any more moves. Let me review what happened.
Looking at the current grid image, I see:
- A black F-shape at the top left (near the light blue line)
- A dark gray F-shape on the right side of the light blue line
- A yellow L-shape at the bottom right.
The goal was to move the F-shapes to match the L-shape or align them somehow. But I've used too many actions and the game is over.
Let me try to restart the level or find a different approach. Maybe the goal is to move the F-shapes to the L-shape by using MOUSE to drag and drop (MOUSE). Let me try MOUSE on the F-shape to see if that moves it to the L-shape.
